summary:
The House-Brackmann grade is utilized for evaluating facial nerve function after surgical interventions aimed at relieving compression near its exits; it has been refined over time but retains primary focus on this specific application. To be concise:

The House-Brackmann scale evaluates facial nerve damage post-surgery by measuring functional recovery across six grades; refinements continue while maintaining centrality to facial nerve assessment. Sentence count: 2
